name: Smith's Union Bar
tagline: Honolulu's oldest bar (1935) — cheap beers, great jukebox, and the unpretentious soul of Chinatown nightlife.
talkStory: Opened in 1935 as a union hall for longshoremen and dockworkers, Smith's is Honolulu's oldest continuously operating bar. The original neon sign and pressed-tin ceiling are intact from pre-war days. During WWII, sailors on shore leave packed it nightly, and that working-class spirit endures. Cash only, no cocktails, no pretension -- the jukebox was curated by regulars and has the best punk-to-Hawaiian-music ratio on the island.
